item.title: Document about her timeline
item.content:encoded: Where the hekk is this???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this article especially the part about loneliness, very dreadful. But the thing I hate about it is the fact that the line “It seems that SCP-2412 is unable to answer questions regarding anomalies in the future, but is somewhat capable of answering questions about them in the past.” Really breaks the article and creates a large logic error. If they can ask her questions about the anomalies in the past then why don’t they ask her “has any anomalies been active in the last one second.” Then get info that way or ask her about the past of other anomalies or people they weren’t able to before. Really good article but the fact that she is very powerful and the foundation doesn’t utilize her to least the full extend I detailed (and other ways to use her that I probably didn’t think of) really bothers me especially because it’ll create a win win situation so it really fucks with the emotional impact of the article. So dislike

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure if this is part of the original myth or just hearsay, but I heard one way she exacted revenge on Apollo for cursing her was by saying flattering true things about him everywhere she went.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Large sad, but good. At least he wanted to come back.

Have a sad upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since the Foundation routinely dehumanizes and objectifies contained anomalies, this would be a meaningless condition. There are never any "humans" involved just "humanoid objects".

There is no canon, so if you want "The Ethics Committee is a joke" you can write it like that, but be aware that most people don't like this interpretation much anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the Foundation's only obligation towards anomalous objects is to contain them. The ethics committee gets involved when humans are in the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't the Ethics Committee or something, want to keep Cassandra happy? No reason to make the robot sad, right?

Maybe some followup on the Internal Conflict questions?

And some more information on what Internal Conflict entails?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love it, but only nitpick is it says:
"SCP-2412 is posed a question about how it was made or where it gets its information."
Followed by:
"In these cases, SCP-2412 will respond with "Warning: Logic Error.""

Then the next paragraph redundantly states:
"It is currently unknown where SCP-2412 gets its information, and when posed a question about this, it will respond with "Warning: Logic Error.""

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The following excerpt is taken from a very early draft of this skip. I wrote this so long ago, I had trouble remembering what some of these were anagrams for, but you guys got 3 of them correct:

SCP-XXXX: Creators: Tesla, Trevithic, Gatling, Foucault.

At this point in the draft I hadn't considered the alternate timeline aspect of things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I would imagine, given the dates, that the Foucault is Léon, of pendulum fame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'Elsat' has a pretty limited pool of possibilities, but I have no way of telling which, if any, are relevant. Anyone have ideas?

Tesla seems the most likely option there. Gatling is a possibility for Tilggan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, Cufalotu is obviously an anagram of Foucault (of the Michel variety), but I'm stalled on the others. 'Elsat' has a pretty limited pool of possibilities, but I have no way of telling which, if any, are relevant. Anyone have ideas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this.

It sort of reminds me of the 1632 series. Was that intentional?

Anyway, great work! Hope it inspires a tale or two! ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope! SCP-1342! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2412: No. Your Foundation will soon discover an old vessel returning from a long voyage."
SCP-2002? That's the closest I can think of.

item.title: Meh
item.content:encoded: You go too far with it. It's not a bad idea but the kind of emotional impact you're going for needs to be more subtle. You need to let the reader feel it for themselves, but instead try to force it down their throats and it comes off as melodramatic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't expect it to be able to speak autonomously - I expected its curse to be "can only talk an answers to questions and thus never form a meaningful relationship with others". You know those times when you meet an old friend and all you can say to each other are "how's your day?" "good" "what are you doing these days?" "well…" - question, answer, question, answer, we should catch up some time, bye - it'd be that sort of depressing. Except that'd be the only way to interact with anyone else, forever.

Also, sad robot girl needs sad robot girl song.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, it's a reference to Frederick the Great.

Frederick's Town = Charles Town / Charleston

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Frederick's Town"

Is that a Fred reference I see?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The alternate timeline stuff and the extra document to go along with it was much more compelling in my opinion. This is great for what it is, but by the end I cared less about the robot than the timeline and history it comes from. No vote from me, but great job writing.

One question though: wouldn't it be simpler to get rid of the first note and include it with the sentence its with, like "…some other languages from its timeline"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is currently unknown where SCP-2412 gets its information, and when posed a question about this, it will respond with "Warning: Logic Error."

That was mentioned when explaining the questions the robot wouldn't answer, even adding a footnote. Repeating it is kinda pointless in my oppinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is also my feels/vote on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The "hook" is in the last interview, where she has that mini-speech. She's gonna be left alone for a while, and she knows it, and there's nothing she can do about it.

I also tried to imply that she had some influence on Carter's thought process, because if she didn't make that speech, Carter would've never requested more interviews. Regardless, she'd still be alone for a good while.

You could also count the alternate timeline as a hook, if you're into that sorta thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. Not bad, but I feel that this lacks a "hook". At the moment, it's just a lonely android that can predict the future. No vote from me, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liking the robot's emotions and alternate timeline, but I think the article needs some work still. The description's got a lot of off-tone passages (starting with calling it a "robot"), not to mention that semicolon should be a colon. I felt the footnotes in particular were off tone as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm torn on this one. On the one hand, it's really, really well written. I actually like that her personality isn't outsized. I imagine her answering questions in a monotone, but that the feelings she's describing are real. The alternate history is also super neat. Where did the idea for Luin come from?

Despite that… I just really don't like the core idea of a skip that's completely omniscient and happy to tell all. It just has way too many implications. I mean, the Foundation is full of scientists, and you've directly stated that it can provide all non-anomalous scientific knowledge. I'm a researcher, and believe me, I would be in there every day.

Sorry if I'm rambling. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Specifically, Cassandra was the daughter of the king of Troy in The Odyssy. She was blessed with theher gift prophesyesy, but cursed such that nobody would belieher. Iirc, she and her entire family are killed in the Greek invasion despite her predictions.

Not a direct correlation, but apt enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cassandra was an oracle in the greek mythology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone else has referred to it as "Cassandra-Bot," what does that mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Cassandra-bot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just a robot that answers questions, and the attempts to imbue it with personality fell flat, so I downvoted. The secondary documentation is much more intriguing and I wish the focus of this article was also on the alternate Germanic timeline, rather than the oracle-bot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially downvoted because the idea didn't really grab me, but you won me back with the worldbuilding in the secondary documentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. Great work, Choiwel. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it enough to upvote. Not the most dramatic scp ever but that's not what it's meant to be, I think. I'm a sucker for the tragic and I thought the interviews were very well done, the end hit me right in the feels.

Couple of nitpicks I noticed:
"This engine provides SCP-2412 with energy to move around, but this cannot be proven"
- I think that ought to say 'appears to' or 'is believed to provide', if it can't be proven.

"one googol[footnote: 10^100 years] year."
- that ought to be years, and either the footnote should be after the word years, or should just read 10^100.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't feel pulled into this. The fact that she can predict the future but unable to change it was drilled into my head far to much, especially in that last interview.

Too much fluff, not enough substance. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 because I personally really like this concept. The way you portrayed the robot was almost relatable for me. The Loneliness, and the item's origins as well as the uncanny accuracy of the robots extrapolations for a lack of a better word.

The part that sealed my upvote for that matter was the robots awareness that even though it knows everything, that knowledge doesn't help it to change the timeline or the extrapolated events in the future at all. Rather it is aware of what the future is but unable to do anything at all.

Take my Upvote man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very special thanks to Cimmerian, Eskobar, Athena_Grey, Wogglebug, Reach, Bennings, Paroxysm, Zolgamax, and anyone else who reviewed this draft in chat.
